evals.prompt: I want to set up a wiki to track my team's AI automation use cases. We mainly build Claude integrations and internal tools.
evals.expected_output: Fully scaffolded wiki in the working directory: wiki-agent.md (operating manual with all 11 required sections), CLAUDE.md (@wiki-agent.md pointer only), CONVENTIONS.md, README.md, index.md, log.md, _templates/ with page-type and entity templates, scripts/ with lint.py/query.py/graph.py, sources/ directory. python3 scripts/lint.py exits clean.
assertions: CLAUDE.md exists and its content is exactly '@wiki-agent.md' (one line, nothing else)
scripts/ contains lint.py, query.py, and render.py
log.md exists and contains an init entry

wiki-agent.md contains all 11 required sections: Directory Structure, Page Type, Absolute Rules, Operations, File Naming, Source File Header Block, Risk Register Format, Wiki Page Frontmatter, index.md Format, log.md Format, Entity and Concept Pages

evals.expected_output: Skill asks both questions (what the wiki is for, what the primary page type is) before touching any files, then scaffolds the same complete structure as eval 0 based on the answers.
assertions: scripts/ contains lint.py, query.py, and render.py
log.md exists and contains an init entry
The skill asked at least one clarifying question before creating any wiki files (check transcript for questions before Write/Bash file creation calls)

evals.prompt: Set up a wiki for this project
evals.expected_output: Skill detects existing CLAUDE.md and appends '@wiki-agent.md' to it rather than overwriting. Original content (including EXISTING_CONTENT_MARKER) is preserved. All other wiki files are created normally. lint passes clean.
assertions: CLAUDE.md contains the string 'EXISTING_CONTENT_MARKER' (original content preserved)
scripts/ contains lint.py, query.py, and render.py
log.md exists and contains an init entry

evals.prompt: Set up a wiki for tracking AI use cases. After scaffolding, ingest one short paper-style page using a brief test text I will paste in chat, then run scripts/render.py.
evals.expected_output: Same wiki structure as eval 0, plus one ingested page in the chosen page-type directory, plus a generated wiki.html at the wiki root containing the page's title, an inline MiniSearch search index, and a D3 graph data block.
assertions: scripts/ contains lint.py, query.py, and render.py
sources/ directory exists and contains at least one ingested file
At least one .md file exists under the chosen page-type directory (e.g. use-cases/, papers/)
wiki.html exists at the wiki root
wiki.html contains the title of the ingested page
